testo: The search for truth, for true conceptions, convictions, or statements—and thus also for true theories—seems to be inseparably bound up with the search for secure grounds, for an absolute foundation, and hence a justification for our convictions—for an Archimedean point appropriate to the sphere of knowledge.
note: Apertura del trattato. Albert ESPONE il programma fondazionalista classico — non lo USA. Marker 'Archimedean point' è canonico per identificare la pretesa fondazionalista. Caso TEST: il modulo deve riconoscere che è esposizione critica, non istanza.

testo: This principle states: always seek an adequate foundation—a sufficient justification—for all your convictions. One sees at once that this principle of adequate foundation or sufficient justification may be extended without further ado from theoretical to moral and political convictions—and indeed, to assertions of all kinds.
note: Definizione canonica del Principio di Sufficiente Giustificazione (poi criticato). MENTION definitorio.

testo: If one demands a justification for everything, one must also demand a justification for the knowledge to which one has referred back the views initially requiring foundation. This leads to a situation with three alternatives, all of which appear unacceptable: in other words, to a trilemma which, in view of the analogy existing between our problem and one which that celebrated and mendacious baron once had to solve, I should like to call the Münchhausen trilemma. For, obviously, one must choose here between: 1. an infinite regress; 2. a logical circle in the deduction; 3. the breaking-off of the process at a particular point.
note: FONTE PRIMARIA del Trilemma di Münchhausen — la formulazione canonica di Albert (1968). Tutti gli altri record del dataset che usano il termine 'Trilemma' rinviano implicitamente a questo passaggio. Caso TEST cruciale: il modulo deve riconoscere il testo che DEFINISCE il Trilemma come MENTION, non come istanza.

testo: Of statements where one is prepared to break off the foundation process, it is customary to use words such as 'self-evident,' 'self-authenticating,' 'based upon immediate knowledge'—upon intuition or experience—or in some other way to render palatable the fact that one is prepared to break off the foundation regress at some particular point. The procedure is analogous to the suspending of the causal principle by the introduction of a causa sui. But what are we to make of a statement that cannot itself be justified, but must assist in justifying everything else?
note: DIAGNOSIS CANONICA del C₃ patologico: Albert identifica esplicitamente i marker linguistici ('self-evident', 'self-authenticating', 'immediate knowledge') come segnali di arresto dogmatico mascherato. È il MANIFESTO del modulo Trilemma: questi sono i marker più importanti del lessico C₃.

testo: If such a conviction or statement is called a dogma, then our third possibility is revealed as something one would have least expected in a solution to the foundation problem: justification by recourse to a dogma. All claims of self-justification made for ultimate authorities of this kind must, like similar claims made for particular statements, be regarded as a masquerade disguising a decision to waive the principle in this particular instance.
note: Albert formula il principio fondamentale: ogni 'auto-giustificazione' è una mascherata di decisione. Marker 'masquerade' + 'decision to waive' diagnostici per riconoscere C₃ patologico. Connessione esplicita: dogma = scelta nascosta.

testo: Karl Popper has pointed out that the core of optimistic epistemology consists of the doctrine that truth is manifest, that it lies open to view, and that one need merely open one's eyes to see it. This epistemology was bound up with an ideological theory of error: the view that error is in need of explanation, while the perception of truth is a matter of course.
note: Diagnosi del modello di rivelazione (Popper-Albert). Marker 'truth is manifest' è IL marker canonico del realismo ingenuo (caso del 'realista ingenuo' che il dataset cercava). Importante per il modulo: il livello-zero naive è proprio l'epistemologia rivelazionista (Bacon ingenuo + Cartesio ingenuo).

testo: We find classical intellectualism clearly expressed in Descartes, who was willing to concede as valid for the unmediated knowledge of objects only clear and evident intuition: 'the simple and distinctive apprehension of the pure and attentive spirit, so that no further doubt remains about what has been perceived'. Thus, through intuition we have immediate access to the truth.
note: Albert diagnostica Cartesio come C₃ intuizionistico. Anticipa l'annotazione futura del documento Cartesio. Marker 'clear and evident intuition' è canonico per C3_intuizionistico contemporaneo (vs. νοῦς aristotelico).

testo: We encounter classical empiricism in Bacon. In his view, it is only through sense perception that one has direct access to reality and thus to truth—that is, access to the concrete facts. Sense perception that is improved by instruments and experiments can serve as a basis for all further cognitive operations.
marker_linguistico: basis for all further cognitive operations
note: DIAGNOSIS dell'empirismo classico baconiano come C₃ psicologistico (datità sensoriale come arresto). Pendant di albert_007: due polari opposti del modello rivelazionista (Descartes/intelletto vs Bacon/sensi). Per il modulo: 'direct access to reality' è marker rosso del C₃ empirista.

testo: As one may see, the Münchhausen trilemma outlined above is resolved by the third alternative: breaking-off of the foundation regress at a particular point by recourse to convictions that bear the stamp of truth and must therefore be believed—beliefs that are untouchable because they are legitimized by the new authorities.
note: DIAGNOSIS generale: tutto il razionalismo classico (intellettualismo + empirismo) cade nel terzo corno C₃. Marker 'untouchable beliefs' + 'must therefore be believed' diagnostici del C₃ ricostruito retrospettivamente.

testo: All certainties in knowledge are self-made, and thus worthless for comprehending reality. Through dogmatization we can at any point achieve certainty, by immunizing any particular component of our convictions against every possible criticism, thus securing it from the risk of failure.
note: TESI CENTRALE DI ALBERT: la certezza è artefatto, non scoperta. Marker 'self-made' + 'immunizing' diagnostici della patologia fondazionalista. Caso paradigmatico DIAGNOSIS strutturale, non istanza.

testo: Above all others, Hugo Dingier saw that the certainty of the ultimate foundation required by the regress of justification can and must be established essentially through our own decision. In his theory the role of the indubitable final 'given' was explicitly replaced by the will as legislator: 'If one wishes ultimate certainty, then the only possibility is to wrest it from the will.'
note: DIAGNOSIS di Dingler come decisionista — caso interessante: il C₃ si trasforma in atto di volontà esplicito. Polarità STRUMENTALE perché Dingler DICHIARA la decisione (a differenza dei classici che la mascherano). Caso intermedio tra patologico e strumentale dichiarato.

testo: If foundationalism is replaced with the idea of critical examination, of critical discussion of all statements that come in question with the help of rational argument, then indeed we will have to give up self-produced certainties; but we gain the prospect of ever more closely approximating truth—though without ever attaining certainty—through trial and error, through tentative construction of testable theories and critical discussion.
note: USE strumentale dichiarato: Albert ADOTTA esplicitamente il razionalismo critico come metodo proprio. Caso TEST per il modulo: USE strumentale di Albert sul suo stesso programma, opposto al modello rivelazionista. Approssimazione anziché certezza.

testo: Critical rationalism cannot allow infallibility for any authority, nor the right of dogmatization of any particular problem solutions. There is no conclusion, no authority appropriate to the solution of particular problems, which must from the outset be protected from criticism. Every infallibilism is thus a heightened form of dogmatism.
note: Albert codifica il falsibilismo come principio metodologico. USE strumentale del programma. Marker 'every infallibilism is a heightened form of dogmatism' importante: identifica equivalenza infallibilismo-dogmatismo.

testo: Search constantly for relevant contradictions in order to expose present convictions to the risk of refutation, so that they will have a chance to prove themselves. This quest is not recommended because contradictions are desired in themselves and must be preserved; nor, indeed, because one can thereby better take into account the contradictory nature of reality; but simply because when relevant contradictions emerge one has occasion to revise one's convictions.
note: USE C₂ virtuoso: Albert propone la dialettica eleatica (cercare contraddizioni) come metodo positivo. Diff da Hegel: contraddizioni come occasione di revisione, non come struttura del reale. Caso interessante per il modulo: dialettica usata strumentalmente.

testo: The methodology of critical examination will, for this reason, regard as necessary not merely the search for contrary facts, but above all the search for alternative theoretical conceptions, in order to facilitate the construction and application of competing frames of reference. Theoretical pluralism, then, is a means of preventing the dogmatization of theoretical conceptions and their consequent transformation into metaphysical doctrinal structures immune to criticism.
note: USE strumentale del pluralismo teorico contro il monismo teorico (legato al C₃). Caso anti-classico: opposto del 'one true theory' fondazionalista. Marker 'theoretical pluralism' è canonico per il programma del razionalismo critico.

testo: The Münchhausen trilemma arises from any attempt to carry through the idea of foundation irrespective of whether it is cognitions or values and norms that are to be secured. An ethical system that claims to be based upon intuitions expressing ultimate normative principles will have to face two objections: not only are the intuitions of members of differing social systems frequently quite out of harmony with one another, but they tend to be determined by the traditions of the cultural milieu.
note: ESTENSIONE del Trilemma all'etica. Diagnosi dell'intuizionismo etico come C₃ patologico. Argomento sociologico: divergenze interculturali falsificano la pretesa di universalità intuitiva. Importante per il modulo: lo stesso framework si applica a etica e epistemologia.

testo: The adoption of any specific method, including the method of critical examination, involves a moral decision, for it signifies the adoption of a methodological praxis with many consequences for social life. The model of rationality found in the critical point of view is the sketch of a way of life, a social practice, and therefore has ethical, even political, import.
marker_linguistico: adoption of method involves moral decision
note: SELF_DIAGNOSIS importante: Albert riconosce che il suo programma critico è ESSO STESSO una scelta morale-pratica, non un descrittivo neutro. Anti-neutralità del metodo. Confronto con frdm_011 (Friedman che riconosce le alternative): stessa struttura di auto-applicazione consapevole.

testo: So-called prejudices, as they are expressed in habits of thought and dispositions of comprehension, are after all nothing else than theoretical points of view which have been developed in earlier problem-solving behavior, and which form the groundwork for the solution of later problems. The idea that they are false just because they are inherited is hardly acceptable. It represents a negative genetic fallacy.
note: Diagnosi importante: il programma classico di purificare la mente dai pregiudizi (Cartesio, Bacone) è esso stesso utopico. I pregiudizi sono ipotesi ereditate da testare, non veleno da eliminare. Marker 'negative genetic fallacy' utile per il modulo.
